description: Financial code should avoid binary floating point for money.
body: Use decimal/minor-unit representations appropriate to the system. Use when finance engineering work touches money movement, ledger entries, reconciliation output, idempotency keys, audit trails, and approval records. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include decimal precision tests, duplicate-submit tests, reconciliation reports, immutable audit evidence, and human approval notes. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves silent balance changes, floating-point money, missing audit trails, regulatory ambiguity, and irreversible financial state, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
